sourceMovie.title: Spartacus
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Spartacus works as a history movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The history and war blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kingdom of Heaven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spartacus clicked for you, Kingdom of Heaven (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and war, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

If Spartacus clicked for you, 300 (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in war and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Spartacus?
faq.answer: Try Kingdom of Heaven, King Arthur, The Great Escape—each shares DNA with Spartacus while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Spartacus?
faq.answer: Spartacus (1960) is best described as History, War, Drama, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Spartacus worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
